data.content:
### Run and View Playwright Traces via CLI

Source: https://context7.com/microsoft/playwright/llms.txt

Demonstrates command-line interface (CLI) commands to run Playwright tests with tracing enabled, open the HTML report, and view specific trace files. This allows for detailed analysis of test execution flow and debugging.

```bash
# Run tests with tracing enabled
npx playwright test --trace on

# Open HTML report with traces
npx playwright show-report

# Open specific trace file
npx playwright show-trace trace.zip
```

--------------------------------

### Open remote Playwright trace via CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

These CLI commands allow for directly opening Playwright traces hosted remotely using a URL. This is particularly useful for debugging CI failures without manual downloads. Commands are provided for JavaScript, Java, Python, and C# environments.

```js
npx playwright show-trace https://example.com/trace.zip
```

```java
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="show-trace https://example.com/trace.zip"
```

```python
playwright show-trace https://example.com/trace.zip
```

```csharp
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 show-trace https://example.com/trace.zip
```

--------------------------------

### Open Playwright trace locally via CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

This set of command-line instructions enables opening a locally saved Playwright trace (`trace.zip`) using the respective Playwright CLI for different programming language environments. The full path to the trace file must be provided.

```js
npx playwright show-trace path/to/trace.zip
```

```java
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="show-trace trace.zip"
```

```python
playwright show-trace trace.zip
```

```csharp
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 show-trace trace.zip
```

--------------------------------

### Analyze Playwright Test Traces

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

Opens the Playwright Trace Viewer to analyze and debug test runs. This command can open without a specific trace file to allow loading via the UI, or directly view a trace file (e.g., trace.zip) or a directory containing trace data.

```bash
npx playwright show-trace [options] [trace]
```

```bash
# Open trace viewer without a specific trace (can load traces via UI)
npx playwright show-trace
```

```bash
# View a trace file
npx playwright show-trace trace.zip
```

```bash
# View trace from directory
npx playwright show-trace trace/
```

--------------------------------

### Bash: Open Playwright Trace File via CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer-intro-csharp.md

This bash command executes the Playwright PowerShell script (`playwright.ps1`) to launch the trace viewer for a specified trace `.zip` file. It's used to inspect test execution details, including DOM snapshots, logs, and network activity, for debugging or analysis. Ensure the path to both the script and the trace file is correct.

```bash
pwsh bin/Debug/net8.0/playwright.ps1 show-trace bin/Debug/net8.0/playwright-traces/PlaywrightTests.ExampleTest.GetStartedLink.zip
```

--------------------------------

### Force Tracing with Command Line (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer-intro-js.md

This command forces Playwright to enable tracing for all tests, overriding the configuration. This is useful for local debugging when UI Mode is not being used. The `--trace on` flag ensures a trace file is generated for every test run.

```bash
npx playwright test --trace on
```

--------------------------------

### Record Playwright Trace with Pytest CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer-intro-java-python.md

This command demonstrates how to enable Playwright tracing when running tests with Pytest. The `--tracing` flag can be set to `on`, `off`, or `retain-on-failure` to control trace recording behavior. Traces are saved as `trace.zip` in the `test-results` directory.

```bash
pytest --tracing on
```

--------------------------------

### Analyze Playwright traces with the Trace Viewer CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

Use this shell command to open the Playwright Trace Viewer GUI. It loads a specified `trace.zip` file, allowing visual inspection of recorded browser actions, DOM, and network activity. This requires the Playwright CLI to be accessible via `npx`.

```sh
npx playwright show-trace trace.zip
```

--------------------------------

### View Recorded Playwright Trace using CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-python.md

This shell command illustrates how to open a previously recorded Playwright trace file (`trace.zip`) using the Playwright command-line interface. Executing this command will launch the interactive Playwright Trace Viewer GUI, allowing for detailed inspection of the recorded browser actions and state.

```sh
playwright show-trace trace.zip
```

--------------------------------

### Open Playwright Trace with CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer-intro-java-python.md

These commands show how to open a previously recorded Playwright trace file (`trace.zip`) using the command-line interface. Examples are provided for both Java (using `mvn exec:java`) and Python (using `playwright show-trace`). This allows interactive inspection of the trace in the Playwright Trace Viewer.

```bash
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="show-trace trace.zip"
```

```bash
playwright show-trace trace.zip
```

--------------------------------

### Debug Playwright Tests with Page Pause and Custom Trace Metadata

Source: https://context7.com/microsoft/playwright/llms.txt

Shows how to pause test execution for interactive debugging using `await page.pause()` and how to attach custom metadata to traces with `test.info().attach()`. These features enhance debuggability and provide additional context within trace files.

```javascript
import { test } from '@playwright/test';

test('debugging with trace', async ({ page }) => {
  // Pause execution for debugging
  await page.pause();

  // Add custom trace metadata
  await test.info().attach('test-data', {
    body: JSON.stringify({ userId: 123 }),
    contentType: 'application/json'
  });

  await page.goto('https://example.com');
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click();

  // Step through with UI Mode: npx playwright test --ui
});
```

--------------------------------

### Record Playwright Trace for Debugging in Python

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-python.md

This Python code demonstrates how to record a Playwright trace using the `BrowserContext.tracing` API. It starts tracing before page navigation, capturing screenshots and snapshots, and then stops and exports the trace to a zip archive for later analysis in the Playwright Trace Viewer. This is useful for debugging Playwright script execution.

```python
browser = chromium.launch()
context = browser.new_context()

# Start tracing before creating / navigating a page.
context.tracing.start(screenshots=True, snapshots=True)

page.goto("https://playwright.dev")

# Stop tracing and export it into a zip archive.
context.tracing.stop(path = "trace.zip")
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright Tracing (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer-intro-js.md

This configuration snippet sets up Playwright to record traces. It enables tracing on the first retry of a test and configures the number of retries based on the environment (CI or local). Traces are essential for debugging failed tests, especially in CI environments.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';
export default defineConfig({
  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0, // set to 2 when running on CI
  // ...
  use: {
    trace: 'on-first-retry', // record traces on first retry of each test
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright to record traces on CI for retried tests

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

This Playwright configuration snippet, typically found in `playwright.config.ts`, sets up automatic trace recording for tests that fail and are retried on continuous integration. By setting `trace: 'on-first-retry'`, a `trace.zip` file is generated for the first retry of a failing test, aiding in CI debugging.

```js
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';
export default defineConfig({
  retries: 1,
  use: {
    trace: 'on-first-retry',
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Opening Playwright Trace Viewer via CLI (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-java.md

Demonstrates the shell command to open the Playwright Trace Viewer for a recorded trace file. This command executes a Maven Java application to launch the GUI for inspecting `trace.zip`.

```sh
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="show-trace trace.zip"
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Playwright Test Code Automatically with Codegen

Source: https://context7.com/microsoft/playwright/llms.txt

Illustrates how to launch Playwright Codegen from the command line, providing a URL to start recording user interactions. Codegen automatically generates Playwright test scripts based on observed actions, speeding up test development.

```bash
# Launch codegen with URL
npx playwright codegen https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Complete Playwright Tracing Workflow

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/api/class-tracing.md

This snippet demonstrates the full lifecycle of Playwright tracing: launching a browser, creating a new context, starting trace recording with screenshots and snapshots enabled, performing page navigation, and finally stopping the trace and saving it to a specified file. It illustrates how to capture a comprehensive log of browser interactions for debugging.

```javascript
const browser = await chromium.launch();
const context = await browser.newContext();
await context.tracing.start({ screenshots: true, snapshots: true });
const page = await context.newPage();
await page.goto('https://playwright.dev');
expect(page.url()).toBe('https://playwright.dev');
await context.tracing.stop({ path: 'trace.zip' });
```

```java
Browser browser = chromium.launch();
BrowserContext context = browser.newContext();
context.tracing().start(new Tracing.StartOptions()
  .setScreenshots(true)
  .setSnapshots(true));
Page page = context.newPage();
page.navigate("https://playwright.dev");
context.tracing().stop(new Tracing.StopOptions()
  .setPath(Paths.get("trace.zip")));
```

```python
browser = await chromium.launch()
context = await browser.new_context()
await context.tracing.start(screenshots=True, snapshots=True)
page = await context.new_page()
await page.goto("https://playwright.dev")
await context.tracing.stop(path = "trace.zip")
```

```python
browser = chromium.launch()
context = browser.new_context()
context.tracing.start(screenshots=True, snapshots=True)
page = context.new_page()
page.goto("https://playwright.dev")
context.tracing.stop(path = "trace.zip")
```

```csharp
using var playwright = await Playwright.CreateAsync();
var browser = await playwright.Chromium.LaunchAsync();
await using var context = await browser.NewContextAsync();
await context.Tracing.StartAsync(new()
{
  Screenshots = true,
  Snapshots = true
});
var page = await context.NewPageAsync();
await page.GotoAsync("https://playwright.dev");
await context.Tracing.StopAsync(new()
{
  Path = "trace.zip"
});
```

--------------------------------

### Create Trace Group with Playwright `test.step` (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/api/class-tracing.md

This JavaScript example illustrates the recommended approach to define a logical group of actions within a Playwright trace using `test.step`. The asynchronous block of code executed within `test.step` will appear as a single, collapsible group in the trace viewer, simplifying debugging and analysis.

```javascript
// use test.step instead
await test.step('Log in', async () => {
  // ...
});
```

--------------------------------

### Describe Locator for Trace Viewer (Python)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-python.md

This Python snippet demonstrates how to use the `Locator.describe` method to add a descriptive name to a locator. This description is then used within the Playwright Trace Viewer, providing enhanced context for debugging and understanding test step execution.

```python
button = page.get_by_test_id("btn-sub").describe("Subscribe button")
button.click()
```

--------------------------------

### Stop Playwright Tracing and Dispose Context (C#)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

This C# code demonstrates how to stop Playwright tracing, conditionally saving the trace file based on a 'TestOk' flag. It dynamically generates the trace file path using current environment and test names. It also ensures proper asynchronous disposal of the base context.

```csharp
await Context.Tracing.StopAsync(new()
{
    Path = !TestOk ? Path.Combine(
        Environment.CurrentDirectory,
        "playwright-traces",
       $"{WithTestNameAttribute.CurrentClassName}.{WithTestNameAttribute.CurrentTestName}.zip"
    ) : null
});
await base.DisposeAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Single Test File CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command runs a specific Playwright test file by providing its path. Useful for isolating and debugging individual test suites.

```bash
# Run a single test file
npx playwright test tests/todo-page.spec.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Codegen with existing User Data Directory

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/codegen.md

This snippet demonstrates how to launch Playwright's `codegen` tool using an existing browser user data directory. This allows `codegen` to access pre-configured browser profiles, including authenticated sessions. A separate user data directory must be created for testing, as default Chrome 136+ profiles cannot be accessed.

```bash js
npx playwright codegen --user-data-dir=/path/to/your/browser/data/ github.com/microsoft/playwright
```

```bash java
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="codegen --user-data-dir=/path/to/your/browser/data/ github.com/microsoft/playwright"
```

```bash python
playwright codegen --user-data-dir=/path/to/your/browser/data/ github.com/microsoft/playwright
```

```bash csharp
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen --user-data-dir=/path/to/your/browser/data/ github.com/microsoft/playwright
```

--------------------------------

### Load Authenticated State with Playwright Codegen

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/codegen.md

Utilize the Playwright 'codegen' command with the '--load-storage' flag to restore a previously saved authentication state from a JSON file. This feature allows you to start a new 'codegen' session directly from a logged-in state, enabling the generation of tests that assume prior authentication without manual re-login.

```bash
npx playwright codegen --load-storage=auth.json github.com/microsoft/playwright
```

```bash
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="codegen --load-storage=auth.json github.com/microsoft/playwright"
```

```bash
playwright codegen --load-storage=auth.json github.com/microsoft/playwright
```

```bash
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen --load-storage=auth.json github.com/microsoft/playwright
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Codegen with Viewport Emulation

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/codegen.md

Execute the Playwright Codegen command to start recording tests while emulating a specific browser viewport size. This ensures that generated tests account for different screen dimensions, providing consistent test conditions across various environments, applicable for JavaScript, Java, Python, and C# Playwright projects.

```bash
npx playwright codegen --viewport-size="800,600" playwright.dev
```

```bash
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="codegen --viewport-size='800,600' playwright.dev"
```

```bash
playwright codegen --viewport-size="800,600" playwright.dev
```

```bash
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen --viewport-size="800,600" playwright.dev
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Codegen Command for Test Generation

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/codegen.md

Execute the Playwright `codegen` command to launch the test generator and open a browser window for interaction. Optionally provide a URL to start recording directly on a specific page. This command is available across various language ecosystems like JavaScript, Java, Python, and C#.

```bash
npx playwright codegen demo.playwright.dev/todomvc
```

```bash
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="codegen demo.playwright.dev/todomvc"
```

```bash
playwright codegen demo.playwright.dev/todomvc
```

```powershell
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen demo.playwright.dev/todomvc
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Playwright Codegen with User Data Directory (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-python.md

This Bash command runs Playwright Codegen, specifying a user data directory to reuse browsing state like authentication across sessions. This allows for persistent browsing context during code generation for easier test creation.

```bash
playwright codegen --user-data-dir=./user-data
```

--------------------------------

### Programmatically Record Playwright Traces in Python and Java

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

These examples illustrate how to initiate and terminate Playwright tracing directly within your Python (async/sync) or Java code. Tracing can be configured to capture screenshots, snapshots, and source code, providing detailed insights into test execution. The collected trace data is then exported to a 'trace.zip' file.

```python
browser = await chromium.launch()
context = await browser.new_context()

# Start tracing before creating / navigating a page.
await context.tracing.start(screenshots=True, snapshots=True, sources=True)

page = await context.new_page()
await page.goto("https://playwright.dev")

# Stop tracing and export it into a zip archive.
await context.tracing.stop(path = "trace.zip")
```

```python
browser = chromium.launch()
context = browser.new_context()

# Start tracing before creating / navigating a page.
context.tracing.start(screenshots=True, snapshots=True, sources=True)

page = context.new_page()
page.goto("https://playwright.dev")

# Stop tracing and export it into a zip archive.
context.tracing.stop(path = "trace.zip")
```

```java
Browser browser = browserType.launch();
BrowserContext context = browser.newContext();

// Start tracing before creating / navigating a page.
context.tracing().start(new Tracing.StartOptions()
  .setScreenshots(true)
  .setSnapshots(true)
  .setSources(true));

Page page = context.newPage();
page.navigate("https://playwright.dev");

// Stop tracing and export it into a zip archive.
context.tracing().stop(new Tracing.StopOptions()
  .setPath(Paths.get("trace.zip")));
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Playwright Codegen with User Data Directory (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-csharp.md

This command demonstrates how to launch Playwright's Codegen tool with a specified user data directory. This allows for the reuse of browsing state, such as authentication, across different sessions, streamlining development and testing workflows.

```bash
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen --user-data-dir=./user-data
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Trace Name and Prefix for Saved Traces

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/api/class-tracing.md

Configure tracing to set a specific title for the trace viewer and a name prefix for intermediate trace files saved in a directory. The `path` option in `stopChunk` is used to specify the final zip file name.

```javascript
await context.tracing.startChunk({ title: 'My Custom Trace', name: 'intermediate_trace' });
// ... actions ...
await context.tracing.stopChunk({ path: 'final_trace.zip' });
```

```csharp
await context.Tracing.StartChunkAsync(new() {
  Title = "My Custom Trace",
  Name = "intermediate_trace"
});
// ... actions ...
await context.Tracing.StopChunkAsync(new() {
  Path = "final_trace.zip"
});
```

```python
await context.tracing.start_chunk(title='My Custom Trace', name='intermediate_trace')
# ... actions ...
await context.tracing.stop_chunk(path='final_trace.zip')
```

```java
context.tracing().startChunk(new Tracing.StartChunkOptions()
  .setTitle("My Custom Trace")
  .setName("intermediate_trace"));
// ... actions ...
context.tracing().stopChunk(new Tracing.StopChunkOptions()
  .setPath(Paths.get("final_trace.zip")));
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Disable Test Parallelization CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command runs Playwright tests sequentially by setting the number of workers to one. Useful for debugging tests that might have race conditions or dependencies.

```bash
npx playwright test --workers=1
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Playwright Locators with Codegen (npm, yarn, pnpm)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/best-practices-js.md

Utilize Playwright's `codegen` command to automatically generate optimized locators for elements on a web page. This tool opens a browser and inspector, allowing interactive selection of elements and providing the best-fit locator, prioritizing roles, text, and test IDs. The examples show how to invoke `codegen` using different Node.js package managers.

```bash
npx playwright codegen playwright.dev
```

```bash
yarn playwright codegen playwright.dev
```

```bash
pnpm exec playwright codegen playwright.dev
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright Trace Recording on Test Failure with MSTest in C#

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

Implements conditional trace recording for MSTest framework that captures traces only on test failures. Uses TestInitialize to begin tracing and TestCleanup to evaluate test outcome, saving the trace zip file only when the test fails, errors, times out, or is aborted. Organizes trace files by fully qualified test class name and test method name.

```csharp
using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
using Microsoft.Playwright;
using Microsoft.Playwright.MSTest;

namespace PlaywrightTests;

[TestClass]
public class ExampleTest : PageTest
{
    [TestInitialize]
    public async Task TestInitialize()
    {
         await Context.Tracing.StartAsync(new()
        {
            Title = $"{TestContext.FullyQualifiedTestClassName}.{TestContext.TestName}",
            Screenshots = true,
            Snapshots = true,
            Sources = true
        });
    }

    [TestCleanup]
    public async Task TestCleanup()
    {
        var failed = new[] { UnitTestOutcome.Failed, UnitTestOutcome.Error, UnitTestOutcome.Timeout, UnitTestOutcome.Aborted }.Contains(TestContext.CurrentTestOutcome);

        await Context.Tracing.StopAsync(new()
        {
            Path = failed ? Path.Combine(
                Environment.CurrentDirectory,
                "playwright-traces",
                $"{TestContext.FullyQualifiedTestClassName}.{TestContext.TestName}.zip"
            ) : null,
        });
    }

    [TestMethod]
    public async Task GetStartedLink()
    {
        // ...
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Code with Playwright CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/library-js.md

This command uses the Playwright CLI to launch a code generation tool, allowing users to record interactions on a specified website (e.g., wikipedia.org) and generate corresponding JavaScript code.

```bash
npx playwright codegen wikipedia.org
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright Codegen with Browser Selection

Source: https://context7.com/microsoft/playwright/llms.txt

Generate Playwright test code by recording user interactions with a specific browser engine (webkit, chromium, or firefox). The codegen command captures actions and generates test scripts automatically.

```bash
npx playwright codegen --browser=webkit https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Preserve Authenticated State with Playwright Codegen

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/codegen.md

Use the Playwright 'codegen' command with the '--save-storage' flag to save the current browser session's authentication state (cookies, localStorage, IndexedDB) to a specified JSON file. This allows you to record an authentication flow once and reuse the saved state for subsequent test generations, avoiding repeated logins.

```bash
npx playwright codegen github.com/microsoft/playwright --save-storage=auth.json
```

```bash
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="codegen github.com/microsoft/playwright  --save-storage=auth.json"
```

```bash
playwright codegen github.com/microsoft/playwright --save-storage=auth.json
```

```bash
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen github.com/microsoft/playwright --save-storage=auth.json
```

--------------------------------

### Debug Playwright Tests with Playwright Inspector (C#)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-csharp.md

These commands activate the Playwright Inspector for debugging .NET tests by setting the `PWDEBUG=1` environment variable. The Inspector provides a powerful tool to step through Playwright API calls and examine locators during test execution.

```bash
PWDEBUG=1 dotnet test
```

```batch
set PWDEBUG=1
dotnet test
```

```powershell
$env:PWDEBUG=1
dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Open remote Playwright trace using trace.playwright.dev URL

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

This URL format demonstrates how to load a remote Playwright trace directly into the `trace.playwright.dev` web viewer by appending the trace file's URL as a query parameter. Users should be mindful of potential CORS restrictions.

```txt
https://trace.playwright.dev/?trace=https://demo.playwright.dev/reports/todomvc/data/e6099cadf79aa753d5500aa9508f9d1dbd87b5ee.zip
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright Trace Recording on Test Failure with NUnit in C#

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

Sets up Playwright trace recording in NUnit tests that only saves traces when tests fail. Uses SetUp to start tracing with screenshots, snapshots, and sources, and TearDown to conditionally save the trace file based on test outcome. The trace is saved to a structured path only when the test result indicates an error or failure.

```csharp
namespace PlaywrightTests;

[Parallelizable(ParallelScope.Self)]
[TestFixture]
public class ExampleTest : PageTest
{
    [SetUp]
    public async Task Setup()
    {
        await Context.Tracing.StartAsync(new()
        {
            Title = $"{TestContext.CurrentContext.Test.ClassName}.{TestContext.CurrentContext.Test.Name}",
            Screenshots = true,
            Snapshots = true,
            Sources = true
        });
    }

    [TearDown]
    public async Task TearDown()
    {
        var failed = TestContext.CurrentContext.Result.Outcome == NUnit.Framework.Interfaces.ResultState.Error
            || TestContext.CurrentContext.Result.Outcome == NUnit.Framework.Interfaces.ResultState.Failure;

        await Context.Tracing.StopAsync(new()
        {
            Path = failed ? Path.Combine(
                TestContext.CurrentContext.WorkDirectory,
                "playwright-traces",
                $"{TestContext.CurrentContext.Test.ClassName}.{TestContext.CurrentContext.Test.Name}.zip"
            ) : null,
        });
    }

    [Test]
    public async Task GetStartedLink()
    {
        // ..
    }
}
```

### Configure Playwright Trace Recording on Test Failure with xUnit in C#

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer.md

Provides xUnit implementation for failure-only trace recording using a custom WithTestName attribute to capture test context. Overrides InitializeAsync to start tracing and DisposeAsync to conditionally save traces based on the TestOk property. Includes a custom BeforeAfterTestAttribute implementation to track current test and class names for trace file naming.

```csharp
using System.Reflection;
using Microsoft.Playwright;
using Microsoft.Playwright.Xunit;
using Xunit.Sdk;

namespace PlaywrightTests;

[WithTestName]
public class UnitTest1 : PageTest
{
    public override async Task InitializeAsync()
    {
        await base.InitializeAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);
        await Context.Tracing.StartAsync(new()
        {
            Title = $"{WithTestNameAttribute.CurrentClassName}.{WithTestNameAttribute.CurrentTestName}",
            Screenshots = true,
            Snapshots = true,
            Sources = true
        });
    }

    public override async Task DisposeAsync()
    {
        await Context.Tracing.StopAsync(new()
        {
            Path = !TestOk ? Path.Combine(
                Environment.CurrentDirectory,
                "playwright-traces",
               $"{WithTestNameAttribute.CurrentClassName}.{WithTestNameAttribute.CurrentTestName}.zip"
            ) : null
        });
        await base.DisposeAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);
    }

    [Fact]
    public async Task GetStartedLink()
    {
        // ...
        await Page.GotoAsync("https://playwright.dev/dotnet/docs/intro");
    }
}

public class WithTestNameAttribute : BeforeAfterTestAttribute
{
    public static string CurrentTestName = string.Empty;
    public static string CurrentClassName = string.Empty;

    public override void Before(MethodInfo methodInfo)
    {
        CurrentTestName = methodInfo.Name;
        CurrentClassName = methodInfo.DeclaringType!.Name;
    }

    public override void After(MethodInfo methodInfo)
    {
    }
}
```

### Debug a specific Playwright test file or line

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/best-practices-js.md

Run a particular Playwright test, identified by its file path and an optional line number, in debug mode. The --debug flag activates the Playwright Inspector for focused troubleshooting.

```bash
npx playwright test example.spec.ts:9 --debug
```

```bash
yarn playwright test example.spec.ts:9 --debug
```

```bash
pnpm exec playwright test example.spec.ts:9 --debug
```

### Record Playwright Trace with Java API

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer-intro-java-python.md

This example demonstrates how to programmatically record a Playwright trace using the Java API. It configures the browser context to start tracing with screenshots, snapshots, and sources enabled before page creation, then stops and saves the trace to `trace.zip`.

```java
Browser browser = browserType.launch();
BrowserContext context = browser.newContext();

// Start tracing before creating / navigating a page.
context.tracing().start(new Tracing.StartOptions()
  .setScreenshots(true)
  .setSnapshots(true)
  .setSources(true));

Page page = context.newPage();
page.navigate("https://playwright.dev");

// Stop tracing and export it into a zip archive.
context.tracing().stop(new Tracing.StopOptions()
  .setPath(Paths.get("trace.zip")));
```

### Debug a specific Playwright test function with Playwright Inspector

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-python.md

Activate the Playwright Inspector to debug a single test function identified by its name using the `-k` flag. This launches the browser and Inspector for granular debugging of that specific test. The `-s` flag is often used to ensure `print` statements are captured in the terminal.

```bash
PWDEBUG=1 pytest -s -k test_get_started_link
```

```batch
set PWDEBUG=1
pytest -s -k test_get_started_link
```

```powershell
$env:PWDEBUG=1
pytest -s -k test_get_started_link
```

--------------------------------

### Group Actions in Trace Viewer with Python

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-python.md

Shows how to visually organize related actions in Playwright's trace viewer using tracing groups. Actions between group and group_end are displayed together as a logical unit, making trace analysis easier by organizing test steps into meaningful sections.

```python
# All actions between group and group_end
# will be shown in the trace viewer as a group.
page.context.tracing.group("Open Playwright.dev > API")
page.goto("https://playwright.dev/")
page.get_by_role("link", name="API").click()
page.context.tracing.group_end()
```

### Emulate Color Schemes with Playwright Codegen

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/codegen.md

This example shows how to launch Playwright's `codegen` in a specific color scheme, such as dark mode. The `--color-scheme` option ensures that the recorded interactions are based on the emulated UI theme. This functionality is supported in JavaScript, Java, Python, and C#.

```js
npx playwright codegen --color-scheme=dark playwright.dev
```

```java
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="codegen --color-scheme=dark playwright.dev"
```

```python
playwright codegen --color-scheme=dark playwright.dev
```

```csharp
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen --color-scheme=dark playwright.dev
```

### Configure Playwright trace recording mode in playwright.config.ts

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-api/class-testoptions.md

This snippet illustrates how to configure trace recording for Playwright tests, setting the mode to 'on-first-retry'. Traces are invaluable debugging tools, capturing browser operations, DOM snapshots, and screenshots to help analyze test failures.

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  use: {
    trace: 'on-first-retry'
  },
});
```

### Enable Playwright trace during local test run

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/best-practices-js.md

This command activates the Playwright trace recording feature for local test executions. Traces capture comprehensive information about each test action, valuable for post-mortem analysis of test failures.

```bash
npx playwright test --trace on
```

```bash
yarn playwright test --trace on
```

```bash
pnpm exec playwright test --trace on
```

### Emulate Mobile Devices with Playwright Codegen

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/codegen.md

This snippet demonstrates how to use Playwright's `codegen` command to record scripts and tests while emulating a specific mobile device. The `--device` option configures the viewport size and user agent, allowing for device-specific testing. It works across JavaScript, Java, Python, and C# Playwright setups.

```js
npx playwright codegen --device="iPhone 13" playwright.dev
```

```java
mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args='codegen --device="iPhone 13" playwright.dev'
```

```python
playwright codegen --device="iPhone 13" playwright.dev
```

```csharp
pwsh bin/Debug/netX/playwright.ps1 codegen --device="iPhone 13" playwright.dev
```

### Launch Playwright Codegen with a persistent user data directory

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

This command launches Playwright's Codegen tool, specifying a user data directory to maintain browsing state between sessions. Using `--user-data-dir` allows reuse of authentication and other session data, which is useful for generating tests that require a logged-in state.

```bash
npx playwright codegen --user-data-dir=./user-data
```

### Record Playwright Trace with Python API

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/trace-viewer-intro-java-python.md

This snippet illustrates how to programmatically start and stop Playwright tracing using the Python API. It shows examples for both async and sync contexts, configuring the trace to include screenshots, snapshots, and source code, then exporting it to `trace.zip`.

```python
browser = await chromium.launch()
context = await browser.new_context()

# Start tracing before creating / navigating a page.
await context.tracing.start(screenshots=True, snapshots=True, sources=True)

page = await context.new_page()
await page.goto("https://playwright.dev")

# Stop tracing and export it into a zip archive.
await context.tracing.stop(path = "trace.zip")
```

```python
browser = chromium.launch()
context = browser.new_context()

# Start tracing before creating / navigating a page.
context.tracing.start(screenshots=True, snapshots=True, sources=True)

page = context.new_page()
page.goto("https://playwright.dev")

# Stop tracing and export it into a zip archive.
context.tracing.stop(path = "trace.zip")
```
